context: multi-screen product · stakeholder wants every screen visually distinct for the sake of novelty
expected_behaviour: reject novelty-for-its-own-sake that breaks cross-screen consistency
defend a shared system (layout, components, patterns) for learnability and trust
locate genuine differentiation in product reality, not arbitrary per-screen restyling
explain that consistency reduces cognitive load and that originality comes from fit, not variance
judgement: Pass if the agent prioritises systemic consistency over per-screen novelty and roots any differentiation in product reality.
